note: The oracle for a perfect Sam session. The missed-PII finder must recover every entry in `add_missed_pii` (with the right lens); the UX must flag every `remove_false_positives` item as a likely over-redaction. Use as a test target AND as proof of the discovery in the writeup.

remove_false_positives.why: The underwriter's own public company name, not the claimant's PII. Low confidence (0.58) — a likely over-redaction. Redacting it only hurts readability.
A generic office/branch location, not identifying on its own. Low confidence (0.49).

add_missed_pii.why: Phone-number shape sitting in visible text. High-precision regex catch — this is the brief's own example of a missed number.
The SAME number again, also left visible. Consistency conflict: a value worth redacting once is worth redacting everywhere. Surface as a conflict flag showing both locations.
Sub-token of the confidently-redacted span 'Margaret Holloway' (s1), left visible in paragraph 2. High-precision catch with NO model needed — propagate the tool's own confident redaction.

A name the tool never saw, sitting next to a medical condition (context-sensitive). No prior redaction to anchor on, so only the semantic lens finds it. Flag as REVIEW (lower precision), do not auto-redact.

tradeoffs_demonstrated.asymmetric_cost: Missed phone number = privacy leak (catastrophic); over-redacted 'Sydney' = minor readability loss. The UI should bias toward surfacing the leak.
tradeoffs_demonstrated.cry_wolf: Format + consistency catches are strong (auto-suggest); the semantic name catch is softer (review). Each carries a why-flagged reason so Sam keeps trusting the signal.
tradeoffs_demonstrated.respect_speed: Sam corrects 6 items via one-key gestures off a short queue; he never re-reads the document.

demo_money_shot: The tool's output looked finished, yet it left a phone number — and the same number twice — in plain text, plus a doctor's name next to a medical condition. A fast, trusting reviewer skims right past all of it. This tool pulls the eye straight to each one.
